conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately, overall?
Tired. It feels like I am dragging myself through the day. I just want to get home and rest.
How long has it felt like you're dragging through the day?
A few months, I guess. It started around the time the kids went back to school. I just didn't have the energy to keep up with the pace anymore.
Is it harder to focus or concentrate than it used to be?
Yeah, it's like my brain is foggy. I'll be sitting at my desk, and I'll just stare at the screen because I can't think of what to do next.
How long does that foggy feeling usually last when you're at your desk?
It depends. Sometimes I get a few emails done and I'm good to go. Other times, I sit there for hours and get nothing done. It's just hit or miss.
How have you been sleeping lately?
Not great. I wake up in the middle of the night and can't get back to sleep. Or I'll fall asleep at 10 pm and wake up at 2 am. It's been weird.
How long has this been happening?
A few weeks, I think. It started when my wife started working the night shift. I just feel like I'm not getting any rest.
Been doing anything you enjoy lately?
Not really. I used to play football with my mates, but I haven't been able to keep up with them for a while. Now I just watch TV or sit around.
What's kept you from playing football with your mates?
I just can't run anymore. I'm 40, I guess. I used to be able to keep up, but now I'm just exhausted after a 30-minute game.
When you think about the next few weeks, what do you expect day to day?
More of the same. Just trying to get through each day without losing my mind. I'm just waiting for the weekend, to be honest.
What does 'losing my mind' look like for you on a typical day?
Just feeling frustrated and stuck. I feel like I'm just going through the motions. It's not like I'm in a crisis or anything, I just feel... stuck.
Do you find it harder to make decisions than before, even small ones?
Yeah. Everything feels like a big choice. Like, should I go to the gym or watch TV? It sounds silly, but it's just hard to decide.
How long has it been since even small choices, like going to the gym, started feeling that way?
A few months, I guess. It's just been getting harder and harder to decide what to do.
Do you feel like you have enough energy for your usual day?
No, not really. I get home from work and I'm done. I don't have the energy to do much of anything.
How long has it been since you've felt too tired to do things after work?
A year or two, maybe. It's just gotten worse over time.
Are you still into the things you used to care about?
Not really. I used to love playing guitar, but I haven't picked it up in years. I just don't have the energy for it anymore.
What else has lost its spark for you lately?
Just about everything. I used to love my job, but it feels like a chore now. I just feel like I'm just going through the motions.
Is there anything weighing on your mind that you wish you could change?
Just the fact that I'm 40 and I'm stuck. I feel like I've missed the boat, you know?
What does being "stuck" feel like for you right now?
It feels like I'm in a rut. I'm not moving forward, and I don't know how to get out of it.
How do you feel about how things have gone for you recently?
Disappointed. I thought I'd have more done by now. But I guess that's just life.
What were you hoping to have done by now?
I don't know. A house, kids, a career. Just the usual stuff. I guess I'm just a bit lost right now.
How's your appetite been?
It's been off. I'm eating, but I'm not really hungry. I just feel like I'm just going through the motions with everything.
Have you noticed any change in your weight because of that?
A bit. I've put on a few pounds. It's not a lot, but it's noticeable.
Have you found yourself getting emotional more easily lately?
Yeah, a bit. I get frustrated and snap at the kids for no reason. I know I'm just tired, but it's hard to stop sometimes.
